fix: always show evidence badge on researcher card regardless of tool use

Some models (e.g. Gemini/Vertex AI) answer the researcher prompt directly
from training data without invoking the web_search tool, leaving
capturedResults empty and never firing OnEvidence — so the badge never
appeared in the UI.

Fix: orchestrator always fires OnEvidence for the researcher role after
Process() returns. If SearchResults is empty, it synthesises a placeholder
card showing what was queried so the badge always renders.

Also: JS isPlaceholder detection uses startsWith('Internal Knowledge')
instead of exact string match so both placeholder sources are caught.

commit.message:
fix: replace O(n) char-by-char buffer allocation with O(log n) doubling

copy_bytes_raw and copy_bytes built destination buffers by appending
one space character at a time in a loop (O(n) string allocations).
Since Novus has no garbage collector, these intermediate strings
exhaust the heap during cumulative operations like nox init, causing
segfaults on linux-amd64.

Replace with exponential doubling (buf = buf + buf) which reduces
allocations from O(n) to O(log n). Fix applied to all platforms:
linux-amd64, linux-arm64, darwin-arm64, windows-x86.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(geocoding): extract clean location text before geocoding

Two bugs caused 'Spring, Texas' to resolve as 'Big Spring, Texas':
1. The entire message content (including prepositions like 'in') was
   passed to Nominatim, making queryLocation 'in spring' instead of
   'spring', so the best-match fallback always chose results[0].
2. Long sentences were passed verbatim with no location extraction.

Fixes:
- New extractLocationText() in pinProcessor: strips hashtag, trailing
  emoji/punctuation, leading prepositions, and for sentence-style
  messages extracts the 'in/at <Location>' fragment.
- geocoding.ts: queryLocation now also strips leading prepositions
  before comparing result names.

Replace broken apt-repo-update action with native reprepro steps

The apt-repo-update@v1.1 action used a debian:buster Docker base image
whose apt repos are now 404 (buster is EOL), causing every run to fail.

Replace with direct reprepro invocations on the ubuntu-latest runner:
- Install reprepro from the Ubuntu apt mirror
- Import the signing key and cache the passphrase in gpg-agent
  via a dummy sign with --pinentry-mode loopback
- Run 'reprepro includedeb' for each .deb in updates/ across all distros
- Clean up updates/ and commit/push the refreshed apt/ tree

Also adds jammy to the distro loop (was previously missing from the
old workflow's repo_supported_distro list despite being in apt/conf/).

* fix: two E2E CI failures — MudSelect click target and Wolverine SignalR injection

Failure 1 (CheckoutPage.cs): SelectAddressByNicknameAsync timed out (30s) because
AddressSelect.Locator('.mud-select-input').ClickAsync() targeted an element that
Playwright's hit-test could not reach in headless Chromium (MudBlazor renders a
transparent overlay on top of .mud-select-input). Reverted to AddressSelect.ClickAsync()
(outer wrapper), which was proven to open the dropdown reliably in the prior CI run.

Failure 2 (CheckoutFlowStepDefinitions.cs + OrderConfirmation.razor):
InvokeMessageAndWaitAsync timed out because OrderStatusChanged and ShipmentStatusChanged
have no local Wolverine handlers — they are SignalR-transport-only messages. Wolverine's
TrackedSession waits for a handler terminal state that never fires. Replaced both calls
with direct IHubContext<StorefrontHub>.SendAsync('ReceiveMessage', ...) injection using a
CloudEvents-shaped payload matching Wolverine's transport format.

Latent bug also fixed: OrderConfirmation.razor's OnSseEvent handler was looking for
'NewStatus' and 'TrackingNumber' (PascalCase) in JsonElement. SignalR serializes via
System.Text.Json camelCase defaults, so properties arrive as 'newStatus'/'trackingNumber'.
This was hidden because InvokeMessageAndWaitAsync always timed out before any message
reached the browser.

* fix: MudSelect Force=true on .mud-select-input, split timeouts to 15s/10s, update docs

Four CI failures all shared the same root cause: SelectAddressByNicknameAsync.
AddressSelect.ClickAsync() (outer wrapper) clicked the bounding-box centre which
landed on the <label> element in headless Chromium — MudBlazor received no open
event, so [role='listbox'] never appeared within 10 seconds.

Fix: Force=true on .mud-select-input bypasses Playwright's hit-test (which was
blocked by MudBlazor's transparent .mud-input-mask overlay) and dispatches the
click directly to the element carrying MudBlazor's @onclick handler.

Also:
- Split MudSelectPopoverTimeoutMs into MudSelectListboxTimeoutMs (15s) and
  MudSelectOptionTimeoutMs (10s) — two distinct wait phases deserve distinct constants
- Updated Pattern 3 and Pitfall 4 in e2e-playwright-testing.md with full
  three-failure-mode history and Force=true rationale

refactor(wayland-demo): reduce runDemo complexity from CC=24 to CC=5

Split monolithic runDemo() function into 7 focused helper functions,
each handling a single phase of the demo lifecycle:

- connectToCompositor(): Establish Wayland connection (CC=4)
- discoverGlobals(): Bind compositor, shm, xdg_wm_base (CC=11)
- createWindow(): Create surface, xdg_surface, toplevel (CC=8)
- createWidgets(): Instantiate button and text input (CC=3)
- renderContent(): Render with software rasterizer (CC=3)
- displayBuffer(): Create shm pool and display (CC=12)
- printFeatureSummary(): Display feature summary (CC=1)

Added demoContext struct to pass shared state cleanly.
Main runDemo() now orchestrates with CC=5.

Impact:
- Cyclomatic complexity: 24 → 5 (79.2% reduction)
- Overall complexity: 31.7 → 7 (77.9% reduction)
- Function count: 418 → 425 (+7 helpers)
- All tests pass (make test-go)

commit.message:
refactor: centralize autosave trigger, move undo/redo to execute_actions

Three changes:

1. Autosave is now triggered once at the end of handle_key() via
   autosave_if_dirty() — checks if buffer is dirty after all actions
   are processed. Removes schedule_autosave() from translate_vim_action.
   This covers edits, undo, redo, task toggle, and any future mutation
   without each handler needing to remember.

2. Action::Undo/Redo handled in execute_actions() alongside other
   editor mutations, not passed through to the TUI.
   handle_vim_command() now just returns the action intent.

3. Tests updated to verify buffer content (the observable behavior)
   rather than action return values (implementation detail).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
